data.body: Work LANDED on main; not a duplicate and not stuck in a worktree. This residual (discovered_from 5e40) was implemented in commit d5733dbc9 '5e40: extend rising-floor convergence to the code-drift re-review axis' (rebar-ticket: 5e40-0fe4-a966-425e, Change-Id I1d7cc6148e...; reachable from origin/main; branch fix/5e40-driftfloor is fully merged, worktree clean). It added src/rebar/llm/plan_review/drift_floor.py (the Pass-3 DRIFT floor for the plan-UNCHANGED + code-DRIFTED axis), the deterministic drop predicate rebar.llm.review_kernel.decide.drift_floor_drop, the required behavioural eval src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-novelty.eval.yaml, and tests/unit/test_plan_review_drift_floor.py. This matches 0916's scope exactly (novel blocking findings on drift-escalation of an unchanged plan; ~32% verdict instability; LLM-surface + behavioural eval). The floor drops a novel finding IFF its citations do NOT intersect the drifted file set and keeps findings citing drifted code. It stayed open only because the commit was trailed with the parent ticket 5e40, not 0916. Closing as completed (work landed under 5e40).
